storybook-addon-tag-badges-alphabadge--d.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-alphabadge--d.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/alphaBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-alphabadge--d.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/alphaBadge" />

# Alpha badge

Use the **alpha** badge to mark that a component or prop is experimental. The badge displays 🔴 with an "Alpha" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `alpha` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['alpha'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-betabadge--do.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-betabadge--do.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/betaBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-betabadge--do.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/betaBadge" />

# Beta badge

Use the **beta** badge to warn that a component or prop is not stable yet. The badge displays 🟡 with a "Beta" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `beta` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['beta'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-codeonlybadge.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-codeonlybadge.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/codeOnlyBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-codeonlybadge.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/codeOnlyBadge" />

# Code-only badge

Use the **code-only** badge for components that only exist in code and not in design. The badge displays 📝 with a "Code Only" tooltip. This badge is hidden in MDX docs.

## Usage

Add the `code-only` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['code-only'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-dangerbadge--.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-dangerbadge--.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/dangerBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-dangerbadge--.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/dangerBadge" />

# Danger badge

Use the **danger** badge for components that require particular attention when configuring them (e.g. security concerns). The badge displays 🚨 with a "Dangerous" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `danger` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['danger'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-deprecatedbad.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-deprecatedbad.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/deprecatedBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-deprecatedbad.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/deprecatedBadge" />

# Deprecated badge

Use the **deprecated** badge for components or props that should be avoided in new code. The badge displays 🗑️ with a "Deprecated" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `deprecated` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['deprecated'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-editorbadge--.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-editorbadge--.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/editorBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-editorbadge--.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/editorBadge" />

# Editor badge

Use the **editor** badge for stories with a live editor. The badge displays ✏️ with an "Editor" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `editor` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['editor'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-integrationba.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/integrationBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-integrationba.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/integrationBadge" />

# Integration badge

Use the **integration** badge for stories that serve as integration tests. The badge displays 🔗 with an "Integration Test" tooltip. It is hidden from the sidebar by default.

## Usage

Add the `integration` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['integration'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

# Keyboard badge

Use the **keyboard** badge for stories with keyboard interactions. The badge displays ⌨️ with a "Keyboard Interaction" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `keyboard` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['keyboard'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-newbadge--doc.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-newbadge--doc.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/newBadge

import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/newBadge" />

# New badge

Use the **new** badge for recently added components or props/features. The badge displays 🆕 with a "New" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `new` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['new'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-outdatedbadge.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-outdatedbadge.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/outdatedBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-outdatedbadge.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/outdatedBadge" />

# Outdated badge

Use the **outdated** badge for components with design changes that were not yet implemented, which can incur extra development costs to your users. The badge displays ⚠️ with an "Outdated" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `outdated` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['outdated'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/propsBadge" />

# Props badge

Use the **props** badge to indicate stories focused on component props. The badge displays 🔧 with a "Props" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `props` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['props'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-rcbadge--docs.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-rcbadge--docs.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/rcBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-rcbadge--docs.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/rcBadge" />

# RC badge

Use the **rc** badge to mark that a component or prop is a release candidate. The badge displays 🔵 with a "Release Candidate" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `rc` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['rc'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-removebadge--.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-removebadge--.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/removeBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-removebadge--.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/removeBadge" />

# Remove badge

Use the **remove** badge (☠️) to indicate that a feature, component, or behavior will be removed in a future version.

- **Next release:** `remove` or `remove:next` (same meaning)
- **Specific version:** `remove:<version>` with the major version number (e.g. `remove:2` for version 2.0.0)

## Usage

For removal in the next release, use `remove` or `remove:next` (equivalent):

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['remove'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

Or:

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['remove:next'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

For removal in a specific version, use a version number:

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['remove:2'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

The badge displays the version in the sidebar and docs (e.g. "☠️ v2" or "☠️" for next).

storybook-addon-tag-badges-snapshotbadge.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-snapshotbadge.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/snapshotBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-snapshotbadge.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/snapshotBadge" />

# Snapshot badge

Use the **snapshot** badge for stories with snapshot tests. The badge displays 📸 with a "Snapshot Test" tooltip. It is shown in the toolbar on the story canvas, not in the sidebar.

## Usage

Add the `snapshot` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['snapshot'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-sourcebadge--.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-sourcebadge--.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/sourceBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-sourcebadge--.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/sourceBadge" />

# Source badge

Use the **source** badge to indicate stories that show source code in the docs. The badge displays `</>` with a "Source Code" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `source` tag to your story and use the `showSource()` decorator so the story's source is visible in the docs.

```tsx
import { showSource } from '#repobuddy/storybook'

export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['source'],
	decorators: [showSource()],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

Stories tagged with `source` will show the `</>` badge in the sidebar and docs.

storybook-addon-tag-badges-todobadge--do.name: Docs

# Todo badge

Use the **todo** badge for stories marked as todo or incomplete. The badge displays 📋 with a "Todo" tooltip.

## Usage

Add the `todo` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['todo'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

storybook-addon-tag-badges-typebadge--do.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-typebadge--do.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/typeBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-typebadge--do.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/typeBadge" />

# Type badge

Use the **type** badge for stories that showcase or document TypeScript types. The badge displays `<T>` with a "TypeScript Types" tooltip. This badge is hidden in MDX docs.

## Usage

Add the `type` tag to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['type'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

Stories tagged with `type` will show the `<T>` badge in the sidebar.

storybook-addon-tag-badges-versionbadge-.name: Docs
storybook-addon-tag-badges-versionbadge-.title: storybook-addon-tag-badges/versionBadge
storybook-addon-tag-badges-versionbadge-.content:
import { Meta } from '@storybook/addon-docs/blocks'

<Meta title="storybook-addon-tag-badges/versionBadge" />

# Version badge

Use the **version** badge to indicate when a component, feature, or property becomes available. The tag format is `version:<semver>` (e.g. `version:1.0`, `version:next`).

## Usage

Add a `version` tag with a semver value to your story.

```tsx
export const YourStory = {
	tags: ['version:1.0'],
	render: () => <YourComponent />
}
```

The badge displays the version number in the sidebar and docs.
